layman_headline: Market fear and geopolitical shocks make this a wait-and-watch moment.
layman_explanation: The crypto market is in 'Fear' mode at 27/100, dragged down by news of a U.S. strike on Iran and uncertainty around Trump's China comments. Bitcoin dropped below $63,000 and most altcoins are bleeding even harder. While Ethereum's RSI is approaching oversold territory, suggesting it could bounce soon, the broader environment is too hostile to jump in right now. We're watching for the dust to settle before committing capital.
layman_action: Stay on the sidelines for now. If Ethereum drops closer to $1,750 and the geopolitical headlines stabilize, that could be a strong buying opportunity. Don't chase anything in a fearful, headline-driven selloff.
expert_reasoning: Ethereum at RSI 35 is the most compelling oversold signal among candidates, and RSI has an 80% win rate in our signal credibility data. However, several factors counsel patience: (1) price is below all major MAs indicating bearish structure, (2) risk/reward to nearest levels is only 0.36x which is terrible for a swing entry, (3) BTC dominance at 56.1% signals capital rotation away from alts, and (4) geopolitical catalysts (Iran strike, China trade tension) create asymmetric downside risk that technicals cannot price in. The Bullish Marubozu candle and +6.5% 5-day momentum are encouraging but insufficient without confirmation of support holding near $1,700. A drop to $1,750 with stabilizing macro conditions would create a much better entry with proper risk/reward. Institutional flows are mixed — Bitcoin ETFs saw $368M inflows over 3 days (bullish for BTC) but this hasn't translated to altcoin strength.
risk_factors: Geopolitical escalation (Iran/China) could trigger a broader risk-off selloff pushing BTC below $60K and ETH below $1,700
BTC dominance at 56.1% and rising means altcoins like ETH could underperform even in a recovery
Fear & Greed at 27 is improving but not yet at extreme fear levels (sub-20) where contrarian buys have highest conviction
watch_list: ETH price action around $1,750-$1,780 support — a bounce here with volume confirms the buy zone
Geopolitical headlines: any de-escalation on Iran/China could trigger a sharp relief rally
Fear & Greed index — if it drops below 20 (extreme fear) that historically marks the best contrarian buy opportunities
market_context: Broad crypto selloff driven by geopolitical shocks — a U.S. strike on Iran and Trump China comments have pushed BTC below $63K. Fear & Greed at 27 is improving slightly from 25 but remains firmly in fear territory, with BTC dominance at 56.1% punishing altcoins disproportionately.
context_banner: Geopolitical risk dominates a fearful market — patience over premature positioning until the macro picture clarifies.

key_signals.description: ETH RSI at 35 is the strongest oversold reading among liquid candidates, and RSI signals have an 80% historical win rate in our trading data — but entry timing needs confirmation.
Bitcoin under $63K after U.S. strike on Iran plus Trump China uncertainty — this geopolitical headline risk overrides bullish technicals and counsels caution.
Bitcoin ETFs added $368M in a 3-day buying streak, showing institutional demand persists — a potential floor for BTC that could stabilize the broader market.
Fear & Greed at 27 improving from 25 — trending in the right direction but not yet at extreme fear levels where contrarian entries have highest expected value.
ETH shows a Bullish Marubozu candle with +6.5% 5-day momentum, but price remains below all major MAs and risk/reward to nearest levels is only 0.36x — insufficient for entry.
